for_planning_node_texts: Let's start with reasons to abolish it.
Another counterargument might be about the fairness of the legal system.
Religious views can play a role too.
I also need to consider international perspectives.
Another point is the psychological impact on those involved in executions.
There's also the question of whether life without parole is a sufficient alternative.
In terms of deterrence, I need to look at studies.

nodes.node_text: Capital punishment is when the government executes someone as punishment for a serious crime, usually murder.
Some countries have it, others don't.

argument_list.matched_argument: Abolishing capital punishment is impossible. It is mostly used today by dictatorial countries and religious monarchies, and they don't obey world norms and suggested human rights

nodes.node_text: One common argument is that it's irreversible, and if there's a mistake, you can't undo an execution.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment is irreversible, there are too many miscarriages of justice to justify retention of the penalty.
capital punishment is too risky as sometimes innocent people are killed. unless it can be known for certain that a person is guilty, capital punishment wiill always present too much of a risk.
capital punishment provides no opportunity for miscarriages of justice to be rectified.
Given that we can almost never guarantee that someone isn't wrongly convicted, we shouldn't have capital punishment because it can't be undone if new evidence comes out.
if someone was found not guilty, they wouldn't be able to bring him back to life after being killed under false accusations
Innocent people may be wrongly convicted and executed.
it could be carried out on someone wrongly convicted

it is inhumane and teaches that violence solves violence
until the justice system is 100% correct all the time, we cannot risk killing someone that may later be proven innocent

It is never possible to determine with 100% certainty that a person is legally guilty of a crime, and capital punishment often punishes innocent people, as we've learned from recent exculpatory DNA evidence in recent years.
it is possible that we could provide capital punishment to an innocent individual.
it's not fool proof enough. the system already mistakenly punishes innocent people at an alarming rate. the risk of an innocent person being put to death is too great.
Just one life mistakenly taken in the name of justice is too many; capital punishment must be abolished.
many people are falsely executed for crimes they didn’t commit, and if this was abolished that wouldn’t happen.
Mistakes are sometimes made in the law - what if someone is killed who is actually innocent?
taking a life is an irreversible decision, and there have been many proven miscarriages of justice in past that could not be remedied due to such punishment.

the most obvious reason to oppose is the danger that an innocent person is killed due to the fact that mistakes are made. witnesses can be wrong in what they saw and lawyers can make a hash of defending a case.
the risk of executing an innocent person, especially in a criminal justice system as flawed as our own, is too high.
the risk of killing even one wrongly accused person is enough to warrant abolishing capital punishment.
there have been instances where the person that faces capital punishment was later found to be not guilty of the crime that they have been accused of.
there is a possibility that an innocent person will be put to death.
too many innocent persons have been discovered on death row, so we should just abolish capital punishment so that we do not punish an innocent person in such a way as can not be reversed.
Unlike prison sentences, the death penalty is irreversible and irreparable
we are seeing to many examples of innocent people being executed, once capital punishment is done we can't take it back.
we should abolish capital punishment because sometimes people are wrongfully accused and once dead cannot bring them back so life in prison is better
we should abolish capital punishment because there is no possibility of correcting a miscarriage of justice where people have died for crimes they did not commit.
You can't take it back. The death penalty is irreversible

nodes.node_text: There have been cases where people were wrongfully convicted and later exonerated through DNA evidence, but if they were already executed, that's a huge problem.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment can potentially lead to the death of innocent men. there are cases where there is strong doubt that the person executed actually carried out the crime.
capital punishment does not allow for people convicted wrongly, for whatever reason, and a posthumous innocence verdict does not make it any less inhumane
capital punishment has already led to innocent people being put to death, we should not allow this to happen.

capital punishment is barbaric, what if an innocent person is killed under these laws, you cannot release them and make restitution, capital punishment makes authorities as guilty of crime as the criminals.
capital punishment is cruel and in some cases people have been punished for crimes they didn't commit
capital punishment is irreversible, there are too many miscarriages of justice to justify retention of the penalty.
capital punishment is too risky as sometimes innocent people are killed. unless it can be known for certain that a person is guilty, capital punishment wiill always present too much of a risk.
capital punishment provides no opportunity for miscarriages of justice to be rectified.
Capital punishment should be abolished because too many innocent people are put to death because they have been wrongly accused.
convictions are being overturned even 30 years later; if these people had been executed it would be too late
Given that we can almost never guarantee that someone isn't wrongly convicted, we shouldn't have capital punishment because it can't be undone if new evidence comes out.
if someone was found not guilty, they wouldn't be able to bring him back to life after being killed under false accusations
Innocent people may be wrongly convicted and executed.
it could be carried out on someone wrongly convicted
it has been proven that some people who were put to death were actually innocent of the crime.

It is never possible to determine with 100% certainty that a person is legally guilty of a crime, and capital punishment often punishes innocent people, as we've learned from recent exculpatory DNA evidence in recent years.
it's not fool proof enough. the system already mistakenly punishes innocent people at an alarming rate. the risk of an innocent person being put to death is too great.

many innocent lives were being taken by this profoundly flawed practice
many innocents have been killed as a result of capital punishment.  even one innocent killed is one too many.

the most obvious reason to oppose is the danger that an innocent person is killed due to the fact that mistakes are made. witnesses can be wrong in what they saw and lawyers can make a hash of defending a case.
the risk of executing an innocent person, especially in a criminal justice system as flawed as our own, is too high.
the risk of killing even one wrongly accused person is enough to warrant abolishing capital punishment.

Unlike prison sentences, the death penalty is irreversible and irreparable
we are seeing to many examples of innocent people being executed, once capital punishment is done we can't take it back.

we cannot always be sure that the correct verdict has been achieved as has been shown in previous cases, thus depriving a person of their life.  it should be abolished to prevent  loss of innocent life.
We have seen too many cases of overturned judgments as new technologies and evidence have come to light, rendering capital punishment inherently problematic.
we should abolish capital punishment as innocent people have been previously sentenced to death
we should abolish capital punishment because sometimes people are wrongfully accused and once dead cannot bring them back so life in prison is better
we should abolish capital punishment because there is no possibility of correcting a miscarriage of justice where people have died for crimes they did not commit.

nodes.node_text: Another argument is that it's more expensive than life imprisonment.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment does not deter crime and is costly.

Capital punishment often comes at a higher cost to the state and taxpayers than simply leaving someone in jail for life, meaning that abolishing capital punishment would save time and pressure on the courts.
keeping inmates on death row is much more costly than life in prison
we should abolish capital punishment because it costs more to kill an individual than it does to house them in prison for life

nodes.node_text: I remember reading that death penalty cases cost more because of the lengthy trials, appeals, and security measures.

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment often comes at a higher cost to the state and taxpayers than simply leaving someone in jail for life, meaning that abolishing capital punishment would save time and pressure on the courts.
capital punishment takes too long and has a long appeal process costing taxpayers money.
we should abolish capital punishment because it costs more to kill an individual than it does to house them in prison for life

nodes.node_text: Taxpayers end up paying more for executions than for keeping someone in prison for life.

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment often comes at a higher cost to the state and taxpayers than simply leaving someone in jail for life, meaning that abolishing capital punishment would save time and pressure on the courts.
keeping inmates on death row is much more costly than life in prison
we should abolish capital punishment because it costs more to kill an individual than it does to house them in prison for life

nodes.node_text: That's a practical reason to abolish it.

argument_list.matched_argument: Death penalty is an inhumane, unjust, unnecessary, and inefficient measure.

nodes.node_text: Some people believe that killing someone as punishment is wrong, regardless of the crime.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment is morally wrong, since it can never be acceptable to take a human life; applying an act of violence as a response to an act of violence is not a solution.
capital punishment should absolutely be abolished because it is inhumane to kill a person no matter how badly they behave. an eye for an eye makes the whole world blind.
killing people is wrong, no matter what crime they committed
killing someone is never okay even if they are a killer.
we should abolish capital punishment because all life is valuable and taking it from anyone is wrong.
we should abolish capital punishment because it is inhumane to kill a human being. an eye for an eye doesn't do us any good. they just need to be changed to a better person, not killed.

nodes.node_text: It's a violation of the right to life, and the state shouldn't be in the business of taking lives.

argument_list.matched_argument: All executions violate the right to life and are a gross affront to human dignity
capital punishment is a massive transgression of the dignity of life, which should have no exceptions.
capital punishment is inhumane and disrespectful to the sanctity of life
Capital punishment is inhumane, as we value the right to life in all societies, yet this action of state violence violates that most fundamental right.
Capital punishment wrongly gives governments the power to take human life.
the death penalty  is a violation of the human right to life
The death penalty goes against our most basic human right - the right to life
The death penalty is incompatible with human rights and human dignity
the right of life muts be preservered

nodes.node_text: There's also the argument that it doesn't deter crime effectively.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment does not deter crime
capital punishment doesn't stop a crime.
Capital punishment has no deterrent effect on crime,
capital punishment is not an effective deterrent to crime and is unnecessarily barbaric.
capital punishment is simply a waste of resources, and it does not serve as an effective deterrent to major crimes.
it doesn't deter crime and it's simply about revenge. the innocence project has exonerated twenty people on death row, and with their limited resources i have no doubt the number is higher.
It has been shown that capital punishment doesn't deter crime, making it useless.
No-one has ever proven with numbers that killing murderers stops other people committing similar crimes
the death penalty has never been proven as a detterent to crime.
the death penalty is not an effective deterrent to crime
There is ample evidence showing that capital punishment does not constitute effective deterrence.
there is no credible evidence that capital punishment acts as a deterrent
There is no credible evidence that the death penalty deters crime more effectively than a prison term
there is no evidence that capital punishment stops crimes
we should abolish capital punishment because there is no evidence that it has any effect in detering criminals.

nodes.node_text: Studies have shown mixed results, but some say that the death penalty doesn't lower murder rates more than life imprisonment does.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment does not deter crime
capital punishment does not deter crime and is costly.
capital punishment doesn't stop a crime.
capital punishment has been proven ineffective in reducing crime rates.
capital punishment has done nothing to actually deter serious crime in this country
Capital punishment has no deterrent effect on crime,
capital punishment is barbaric, ties up a lot of resources, and doesn't really act as a deterrent to major crimes.
capital punishment is just ineffective. most states that still use it as punishment actually have higher murder rates and crime than those that do not.

there is no evidence that capital punishment stops crimes

nodes.node_text: On the other side, people who support capital punishment argue that it provides justice for victims and their families.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment allows for equal justice type punishment and a means for the victim's family members to have some type of closure to such a horrible episode in their lives.
capital punishment allows for victims' families to get justice equal to the crime committed (usually murder).
capital punishment provides a firm sense of closure for victim's families and the lack thereof may harm them.
Capital punishment provides closure for victim's families who can be reassured that the perpetrator can literally never harm anyone again.
Capital punishment provides permanent closure to victims and their families who have experienced immense trauma and have been subjected to intense pain by the criminal.
capital punishment provides the best outcome for the families of victims. they can rest easier knowing that the person who harmed their family person can no longer hurt anyone.
strict capital punishment brings justice and closure to the victim's family
The death penalty can provide families of victims with some closure, which may help them to deal with their suffering.
the death penalty gives the victims of the worst crimes justice and takes away the option that they could be released to commit these crimes again
the death penalty is very important as it can deter people from murder.   it saves a lot of taxpayer money.   it gives victims families a little more relief and justice.
the severity of a crime that leads to capital punishment is surely a just result for the victim's family.

we should not abolish capital punishment because it brings fair closure for those that are suffering because the criminal still lives.  in most cases because their loved one was murdered by the criminal.
When someone dear to you is horribly mudered and mutilated, you understandably want retribution and justice, and in extreme cases you deserve it

nodes.node_text: They believe that some crimes are so heinous that the only appropriate punishment is death.

some crimes are so despicable that death is the only appropriate punishment
Some crimes are so heinous and inherently wrong that they demand death penalties
some crimes are so heinous that the only just punishment is the death penalty.
We should not abolish capital punishment as it is the only fitting punishment for some of the most heinous crimes that people still commit.
we should not abolish capital punishment because there are some heinous crimes which deserve the death penalty.

nodes.node_text: Also, there's the idea that it prevents the criminal from ever committing another crime.

Capital punishment is beneficial to society: you are removing a murderer from society who could possibly kill again, even while in prison.
capital punishment is the best method to ensure that a murderer does not kill again
capital punishment is the best way of stopping a murderer from killing again.
Capital punishment provides closure for victim's families who can be reassured that the perpetrator can literally never harm anyone again.
Giving a killer the death sentence will stop them  doing it again
some people are so evil that they need the ultimate punishment so they can't ever harm any one again.
we should not abolish capital punishment because it is the best way of ensuring that murderers do not kill again.
When the death penalty is performed, there is no chance for the individual to escape and commit another crime

killers need to be eliminated for the safty of society.

nodes.node_text: Supporters might say that with modern technology, like DNA evidence, the risk of wrongful convictions is lower.

argument_list.matched_argument: It is never possible to determine with 100% certainty that a person is legally guilty of a crime, and capital punishment often punishes innocent people, as we've learned from recent exculpatory DNA evidence in recent years.
We have seen too many cases of overturned judgments as new technologies and evidence have come to light, rendering capital punishment inherently problematic.
With the creation of DNA testing and other new forensic analysis, we can eliminate uncertainty in the guilt of people who deserve the death penalty.

nodes.node_text: So if the system is reliable, then capital punishment is justified for the worst offenders.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment is an effective deterrent to major crimes, and should only be used for the very worst of crimes.
capital punishment is needed for the most heinous criminnals.
capital punishment is the only acceptable punishment for some heinous crimes because they are so evil, life in prison is not enough.
Capital punishment should be kept, in order to be used in the most extreme and horrible crimes. Not so much as a deterrant but as a way to acheive  a sort of justice.
We should not abolish capital punishment as it is the only fitting punishment for some of the most heinous crimes that people still commit.
we should not abolish capital punishment because there are some heinous crimes which deserve the death penalty.
Which crimes get capital punishment can be narrowed down and reformed, but capital punishment should still exist because some crimes are just too horrendous to allow the perpetrator to stay alive.

nodes.node_text: They might also argue that it's a necessary tool for prosecutors to use in extreme cases, even if it's rarely applied.

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment should be kept, in order to be used in the most extreme and horrible crimes. Not so much as a deterrant but as a way to acheive  a sort of justice.
if reserved for extreme cases, capital punishment can send a strong message that society condemns such crimes.
Which crimes get capital punishment can be narrowed down and reformed, but capital punishment should still exist because some crimes are just too horrendous to allow the perpetrator to stay alive.

nodes.node_text: But then again, even with DNA evidence, there's still a possibility of human error or misconduct in the legal process.

argument_list.matched_argument: It is never possible to determine with 100% certainty that a person is legally guilty of a crime, and capital punishment often punishes innocent people, as we've learned from recent exculpatory DNA evidence in recent years.
it's not fool proof enough. the system already mistakenly punishes innocent people at an alarming rate. the risk of an innocent person being put to death is too great.

the most obvious reason to oppose is the danger that an innocent person is killed due to the fact that mistakes are made. witnesses can be wrong in what they saw and lawyers can make a hash of defending a case.
We have seen too many cases of overturned judgments as new technologies and evidence have come to light, rendering capital punishment inherently problematic.

nodes.node_text: Racial and socioeconomic disparities in who gets the death penalty are also a concern.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment can suffer from social or racial bias and in fact be used as a weapon against a certain section of society
Capital punishment has historically been unfairly used more against minorities and the weak people in society, and I believe it's true today
capital punishment is applied unfairly among the different races, so it should be replaced with a better form of punishment that treats everyone equally.
Capital punishment is asymmetrically enforced against people of color and religious minorities.
Capital punishment is just another way to oppress and control minorities and certain groups which undermine the mainstreem perceptions
capital punishments targets mostly men of color and sometimes even innocent people are put to death.  you cannot make reparations to a person after you have killed them.

In places where capital punishment is legal, it is often used in discriminatory ways against minority groups, and therefore we should abolish it to prevent disproportionate punishment against the most vulnerable people in society.
the death penalty is a racially biased punishment, and also ineffective in deterring crime
The death penalty is disproportionally used against the poor and minorities.

nodes.node_text: Statistics show that minorities and those who can't afford good lawyers are more likely to be sentenced to death.

argument_list.matched_argument: Capital punishment has historically been unfairly used more against minorities and the weak people in society, and I believe it's true today
capital punishment is applied unfairly among the different races, so it should be replaced with a better form of punishment that treats everyone equally.
Capital punishment is asymmetrically enforced against people of color and religious minorities.

In places where capital punishment is legal, it is often used in discriminatory ways against minority groups, and therefore we should abolish it to prevent disproportionate punishment against the most vulnerable people in society.
the death penalty is a racially biased punishment, and also ineffective in deterring crime
The death penalty is disproportionally used against the poor and minorities.

nodes.node_text: That's a significant issue related to fairness and justice.

argument_list.matched_argument: A state that outlaws murder cannot then turn around and murder prisoners in the name of the law. It is perverse and unjust.
Death penalty is an inhumane, unjust, unnecessary, and inefficient measure.

nodes.node_text: Some religions teach that life is sacred and only God can take it away, while others might support retributive justice.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment is against god's will.
capital punishment kills people. god said "thou shall not kill!"
capital punishment should be abolished because man is not the one that should be making the decision to take a life, but leave that to the all mighty god.
only god decides who lives and who dies, capital punishment should not exist.
people are not the one's to determine who should live or die only god.
we should abolish capital punishment because in term of religious view, god is the author of life and death so only god can decide who die and who lives when times comes.

nodes.node_text: This can influence people's opinions on the death penalty.
Many countries have abolished capital punishment, seeing it as a human rights violation.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment breaks the human rights of the individuals being punished.
capital punishment is inhumane and completely throws out human rights, which even prisoners are entitled to.
Capital punishment is inhumane, as we value the right to life in all societies, yet this action of state violence violates that most fundamental right.
No national interest can justify human rights violations such as the death penalty
the death penalty  is a violation of the human right to life
The death penalty goes against our most basic human right - the right to life
the death penalty is a setback to civility and human rights
The death penalty is incompatible with human rights and human dignity

nodes.node_text: The trend is towards abolition, which might suggest that it's an outdated practice.

argument_list.matched_argument: capital punishment is a barbaric and inhumane practice that is outdated.
capital punishment is a barbaric practice that is outdated and shouldn't exist in a civilized society.
Capital punishment is a cruel, outdated approach that must be abolished in a civilized society
capital punishment is an outdated & cruel form of punishment
capital punishment is an outdated means of punishment.  better and more productive forms of punishment now exist.
capital punishment is an outdated system that needs an upheaval.
